reasoning: The current CDC Level 2 Yellow Fever travel advisory (as of mid-April 2025) is strictly for South American countries (Brazil, Bolivia, Colombia, and Peru), with new cases localized to Guyana and projected spread to Argentina and Paraguay. No North American countries have active cases or are part of this advisory. Yellow fever outbreaks typically spread via sylvatic cycles or specific urban conditions, so the likelihood of an outbreak springing up in a North American country (such as Panama or Mexico) and triggering a brand-new CDC travel advisory within the five-week window between the April 24 knowledge cutoff and the June 1 resolution date is extremely low. Therefore, the probability is near the minimum allowed value.
batch_queries: Will any North American country be listed as having a CDC yellow fever travel advisory on June 1, 2025?
